reforms.description: The Swedish government has implemented fiscal relief measures to stimulate industrial investment in R&D, allowing firms to deduct one-third of their R&D personnel expenditures from taxable profits. This shift in industrial policy focuses on promoting innovation and R&D efforts in industry, providing direct support and fiscal incentives for technology-related expenditures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fiscal relief and direct support for R&D are designed to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The fiscal relief measures and support for R&D have been implemented as part of the government's industrial policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in industrial policy towards supporting innovation and R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The priority of policy has since 1982 been shifted towards promotion of forward-looking transformation of industries within the context of macroeconomic policies which create the appropriate environment for the healthy development of industry.

reforms.description: The government aims to promote small technology-based firms by extending networks for technology and marketing services, enhancing cooperation between universities and industry, and providing adequate information and training at local and regional levels.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering small technology-based firms, the initiative is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to support small technology-based firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to support the startup ecosystem, although it is not as transformative as the fiscal relief measures.
reforms.source_quote: Promotion of small technology-based firms, which would seem to require an extension of the networks for technology and marketing services, better cooperation between universities and industry, and provision of adequate information and training at local and regional level.

reforms.description: The government plans to adapt the research system to reinforce the scientific base of new technologies through timely adjustments of R&D efforts by universities and the public sector. This includes the initiation of a National Programme on Information Technology, which aims to better integrate support for basic and applied research, knowledge propagation, and training in information technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The integration of support for research and training in information technologies is expected to enhance the technological capabilities of the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans for the National Programme on Information Technology.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to adapt the research system but is not a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The adaption of the research system to reinforce the scientific base of the new technologies, which would, in particular, involve appropriate measures for a timely adjustment of the R&D efforts by universities and the public sector.

reforms.description: The government has increased the educational capacity of technical schools to address the shortage of qualified technical personnel in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the educational capacity of technical schools is expected to enhance the supply of skilled personnel, supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in educational capacity has been implemented to address personnel shortages.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important, this reform is more of an incremental adjustment rather than a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Government has, however, increased the educational capacity of technical schools which may reduce the shortage of these types of personnel in the medium term.

reforms.description: The renewal funds approved by Parliament are designated for education, research, and development work inside firms, with the amount deposited in the fund equaling 10 percent of profits exceeding Kr 0.5 million in 1985.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The allocation of funds for education, research, and development within firms is expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The renewal funds have been approved and are in use for R&D within firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of renewal funds is a significant but not transformative reform.
reforms.source_quote: The so-called renewal funds approved by Parliament. Funds are to be used for education, research and development work inside firms.
